query: How many cruise ships have come to the city?

response.headline: Cruise Arrivals from Germany Nearly Quadrupled in 18 Years
response.lede: Every summer, cruise ships bring thousands of passengers from all over the world to Faxaflóahafnir. But no group has grown as quickly as visitors from Germany. The numbers nearly quadrupled from 13,000 in 2002 to almost 49,000 in 2019.

story_blocks.body: Germany went from sending about 13,000 passengers in 2002 to almost 49,000 in 2019—a dramatic rise for any single country.

story_blocks.heading: German Cruise Passenger Numbers Soared
story_blocks.body: If you look at the chart below, you’ll see the sharp climb in German cruise ship passengers over the years. After some steady growth, the total leapt even higher after 2012, reaching a peak in 2019. This jump means that more cruises coming through our ports now include stops for German holidaymakers—and their numbers have outpaced all other groups.

story_blocks.heading: Holland’s Numbers Rose Then Slipped
story_blocks.body: The next chart shows how passengers from Holland also grew, but their trend is a little different. After peaking at over 1,400 passengers in 2008, numbers slipped back to just under 1,200 in 2019—a drop of about one-sixth. While still well above the early 2000s, their growth has not kept pace with Germany.

story_blocks.heading: Russian Visitors Shrinking Despite an Early Peak
story_blocks.body: Russian cruise passengers tell a different story in the chart below. Numbers started high in 2002, peaked at 660 in 2005, but steadily declined to just 330 by 2019. That's about a 40% drop from where they started, showing that not every nationality is following the same trend.

story_blocks.heading: Swiss Arrivals Peaked in 2012
story_blocks.body: Looking at arrivals from Switzerland, you'll see a sharp rise to just over 1,600 passengers in 2012—their highest point on record. But after that, the numbers began to drop. Even so, the 2019 total was still about five times what it was in 2002.

story_blocks.heading: Austrian Arrivals Are Way Up Too
story_blocks.body: For Austrian visitors, growth was steady with a notable peak at nearly 1,400 passengers in 2012. Numbers stayed well above early 2000s levels in recent years, showing that many more Austrians are stopping here during their cruises compared to the past.

story_blocks.heading: What This Means for Residents
story_blocks.body: This surge in cruise passengers, especially from Germany and other European countries, has brought more visitors—and more activity—to our city. While some groups like the Russians are sending fewer tourists, the overall rise is clear. It means busier summers around the harbor, new business for locals, and more international ties with every docking cruise ship.

response.data_note: This information comes from official port authorities and shows yearly counts of cruise ship passengers by nationality passing through Faxaflóahafnir, from 2002 through 2019. It tracks groups from several countries, not just overall totals. The numbers reflect only those stopping by cruise ship, not all tourists. Note that the most recent year included is 2019, so recent changes are not captured. Also, the portal doesn’t say what licence these numbers are published under.
response.followup_question: How has air travel through the city’s airport changed since 2002?
